questions.q: Which fastidious, mustachioed Belgian detective is Agatha Christie's most famous creation?
Which elderly amateur sleuth from St. Mary Mead is Christie's other great detective?
Which Christie novel takes place aboard a transcontinental train stranded in a snowdrift?
Which Christie novel is often cited as the best-selling mystery ever written?
Which Christie novel has Poirot solving a murder on a steamer along a famous African river?
Hercule Poirot is famously from which country?
In 1971, Agatha Christie was honored by the Queen with which title?
choices: Dame Commander of the British Empire

questions.q: In what year was Agatha Christie born?
Which Agatha Christie play is the longest-running play in theatrical history?
What was the profession of Agatha Christie's second husband, Max Mallowan?
In which novel does Miss Marple make her first appearance?
What is the title of Agatha Christie's first published novel?
choices: The Man in the Brown Suit

questions.q: Which character is the trusted companion and friend of Hercule Poirot in many of his cases?
What is the name of the young married couple who appear as detectives in several Agatha Christie novels?
Which actress played Miss Marple in the 1960s film 'Murder, She Said'?
What is the name of Agatha Christie's crime writer character who appears as a detective in several novels?
Which British actor portrayed Hercule Poirot in the long-running television series that began in 1989?
In which Agatha Christie novel do Tommy and Tuppence investigate a mysterious organization called 'N'?
choices: By the Pricking of My Thumbs

questions.q: What is the title of Agatha Christie's autobiography, published after her death?
Which Agatha Christie novel centers on the murder of a wealthy businessman surrounded by his eccentric family?
In which country was Agatha Christie born?
What is the profession of Superintendent Battle, a detective who appears in multiple Agatha Christie novels?
Which Agatha Christie novel features a murder mystery centered on a vicar's household?
In what English town was Agatha Christie born?
Which detective duo, a young married couple, first appeared in the novel 'The Secret Adversary'?
Christie's 'The Murder of Roger Ackroyd' (1926) shocked readers because of what narrative choice?
Christie published one novel under a pseudonym before revealing her identity. Which pseudonym was it?
The 1974 film 'Murder on the Orient Express' was directed by which British filmmaker?
Which Agatha Christie novel was adapted into a 1945 film titled 'The Body in the Library' and introduces Miss Marple's detective methods?
In 'Five Little Pigs', Hercule Poirot investigates a murder that occurred how many years before the events of the novel?
In 'Sleeping Murder', the final Miss Marple novel to be published, what crime does Miss Marple help solve in her old age?
choices: A decades-old poisoning case in a country house
A murder committed during World War I that was never prosecuted
A recent murder connected to a house the protagonist recently purchased
A serial killer operating in her village

questions.q: In 'The ABC Murders', what unique method does the murderer use to select their victims?
choices: Victims whose names begin with consecutive letters of the alphabet
Victims living at addresses matching alphabetical railroad stations
Victims chosen from an alphabetical directory
Victims corresponding to letters hidden in newspaper classified ads

questions.q: What is the actual murder weapon in 'Evil Under the Sun', disguised by the detective work of Hercule Poirot?
choices: A poisoned cocktail administered at a beach party
A razor blade hidden in a beach towel
A sharp stone used during a bathing expedition
Strangulation with a rope painted to blend with surroundings

questions.q: Which Agatha Christie short story collection features her detective Harley Quin and his ability to solve crimes through mysterious interventions?
In 'Curtain: Poirot's Last Case', what method does Hercule Poirot use to bring the murderer to justice?
choices: He orchestrates the murderer's suicide to prevent further deaths
He stages a elaborate trap during a dinner party
He arranges for the criminal to confess to Scotland Yard detectives
He manipulates events so the criminal is caught in a act of murder

questions.q: What is the setting and primary mystery in 'Dead Man's Folly', one of Agatha Christie's later Miss Marple novels?
choices: A village fete murder game that becomes all too real
A academic conference where poisoning occurs
A theater production where an actor is murdered
A country house murder during a weekend gathering

questions.q: In 'Towards Zero', which recurring detective character is introduced as a young naval officer connected to the central mystery?
In 'The Pale Horse', what unconventional element of the plot involves a list of names and a village of reputed witches?
choices: A murder conspiracy disguised as supernatural killings
Actual supernatural occurrences investigated by Poirot
A blackmail scheme involving fortune tellers

questions.q: What is the chronological relationship between 'Curtain: Poirot's Last Case' and 'Sleeping Murder: Miss Marple's Last Case' in terms of when Agatha Christie wrote them?
choices: Both were written during World War II as her final works
Curtain was written first; Sleeping Murder written years later
Sleeping Murder was written first during wartime; Curtain written later
They were written simultaneously as companion pieces

questions.q: In 'One, Two, Buckle My Shoe', what profession of the murder victim creates the initial misdirection in Hercule Poirot's investigation?
choices: He is a dentist whose dental work becomes crucial evidence
He is a banker whose financial records are examined
He is a politician whose scandals are revealed
He is a jeweler whose valuables are stolen

questions.q: In 'A Murder is Announced', Miss Marple arrives in the village of Chipping Cleghorn to investigate after witnessing what unusual event?
choices: A murder announcement published in the local newspaper
A suspicious house fire at the local manor
A guest lecturer at the village discussing crime
She is renting a cottage and becomes involved by accident

questions.q: What is the central mechanism of deception in 'The Mirror Crack'd from Side to Side' involving a Hollywood actress and a decades-old incident?
choices: A poisoning meant for one victim accidentally kills another due to mistaken identity
A actress stages murders to cover up her true crime
A case of misidentification involving twins
A revenge plot involving switched medications

questions.q: In 'Witness for the Prosecution', what shocking revelation occurs regarding the apparent victim's wife during the trial?
choices: She is revealed to be an entirely different person assuming a false identity
Her testimony contradicts the defendant's account in a crucial way
She confesses to orchestrating the crime
She is proven to be the actual murderer, not her husband

questions.q: Agatha Christie's play 'The Mousetrap' opened in London's West End in what year?
In 'The Murder at the Vicarage,' Miss Marple's first appearance, what is her relationship to the vicar of St. Mary Mead?
choices: She is his elderly parishioner and neighbor

questions.q: Which Agatha Christie novel explicitly takes its plot from the dramatic structure of 'And Then There Were None' but relocates the setting to a country house and adds psychological complexity through family dynamics?
Agatha Christie's second husband, Max Mallowan, conducted archaeological excavations in which region of the Middle East?
Captain Arthur Hastings, the recurring friend and associate of Hercule Poirot, first meets Poirot in which novel?
Which Agatha Christie novel published in 1926 features the character Superintendent Battle investigating a jewel heist and murder at a grand estate?
choices: The Man in the Brown Suit

questions.q: In 'The Secret Adversary,' Tommy and Tuppence Beresford are hired to locate a missing woman known by what code name?
Agatha Christie was appointed Dame Commander of the Order of the British Empire in which year?
Tommy and Tuppence — the husband-and-wife duo — first meet in which Christie novel?
choices: By the Pricking of My Thumbs

questions.q: Which English seaside town was Agatha Christie's birthplace and lifelong holiday retreat?
Which Christie short-story detective presents himself as a 'psychological consultant' for unhappy people?
In December 1926, Christie famously did what, making national headlines in Britain?
The 1974 film 'Murder on the Orient Express' cast which actor as Hercule Poirot?
Which Agatha Christie novel features a murder taking place during a snowstorm in a country house and is often cited as one of her masterpieces?
In 'The ABC Murders,' what symbolic element does the killer leave at each crime scene?
In 'And Then There Were None,' how many people are invited to the island at the story's beginning?
Which 1974 film adaptation of an Agatha Christie novel features a star-studded cast including Albert Finney and Lauren Bacall?
What is the real name of the character known as Ariadne Oliver, Agatha Christie's second-most famous detective creation?
choices: Her full name is Ariadne Jane Oliver

questions.q: In which novel do Miss Marple and Hercule Poirot appear together?
Which Agatha Christie novel features a character poisoned by thallium and is set in a family home called 'The Hollow'?
What is the setting of the Miss Marple mystery 'A Murder is Announced'?
How many times was Agatha Christie's play 'The Mousetrap' stated to have been performed in its original West End run before closure?
In 'Cards on the Table,' what is the profession of the victim, Shaitana?
Which detective character created by Agatha Christie is described as having 'little grey cells'?
In 'Five Little Pigs,' how is the murder victim, Amyas Crale, killed?
In 'Hallowe'en Party,' a young girl is murdered after claiming to have witnessed a crime. What is this initial claim about?
Which Agatha Christie novel was adapted into a 2017 film starring Kenneth Branagh as Hercule Poirot?
Which Agatha Christie novel features the iconic scenario of ten strangers isolated on an island, each accused of a past crime?
choices: The Mystery of the Blue Train
